verses.latin_vulgate: Transierunt igitur tres anni absque bello inter Syriam et Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּשְׁב֖וּ שָׁלֹ֣שׁ שָׁנִ֑ים אֵ֚ין מִלְחָמָ֔ה בֵּ֥ין אֲרָ֖ם וּבֵ֥ין יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And there passed three years without war between Syria and Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he olivat kolme ajastaikaa levossa, ettei ollut sotaa Syrialaisten ja Israelin vaiheilla.
verses.king_james: And they continued three years without war between Syria and Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: In anno autem tertio, descendit Josaphat rex Juda ad regem Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֖י בַּשָּׁנָ֣ה הַשְּׁלִישִׁ֑ית וַיֵּ֛רֶד יְהוֹשָׁפָ֥ט מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֖ה אֶל־מֶ֥לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And in the third year, Josaphat, king of Juda, came down to the king of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kolmantena vuonna meni Josaphat Juudan kuningas Israelin kuninkaan tykö,
verses.king_james: And it came to pass in the third year, that Jehoshaphat the king of Judah came down to the king of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: (Dixitque rex Israël ad servos suos : Ignoratis quod nostra sit Ramoth Galaad, et negligimus tollere eam de manu regis Syriæ ?)
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר מֶֽלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ אֶל־עֲבָדָ֔יו הַיְדַעְתֶּ֕ם כִּי־לָ֖נוּ רָמֹ֣ת גִּלְעָ֑ד וַאֲנַ֣חְנוּ מַחְשִׁ֔ים מִקַּ֣חַת אֹתָ֔הּ מִיַּ֖ד מֶ֥לֶךְ אֲרָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: (And the king of Israel said to his servants: Know ye not that Ramoth Galaad is ours, and we neglect to take it out of the hand of the king of Syria?)
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin kuningas sanoi palvelioillensa: ettekö te tiedä Gileadin Ramotia olevan meidän omamme? ja me istumme ääneti ja emme ota sitä Syrian kuninkaan kädestä?
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel said unto his servants, Know ye that Ramoth in Gilead is ours, and we be still, and take it not out of the hand of the king of Syria?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait ad Josaphat : Veniesne mecum ad præliandum in Ramoth Galaad ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־יְה֣וֹשָׁפָ֔ט הֲתֵלֵ֥ךְ אִתִּ֛י לַמִּלְחָמָ֖ה רָמֹ֣ת גִּלְעָ֑ד וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יְהֽוֹשָׁפָט֙ אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל כָּמ֧וֹנִי כָמ֛וֹךָ כְּעַמִּ֥י כְעַמֶּ֖ךָ כְּסוּסַ֥י כְּסוּסֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to Josaphat: Wilt thou come with me to battle to Ramoth Galaad?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi Josaphatille: tuletkos minun kanssani sotimaan Gileadin Ramotia vastaan? Josaphat sanoi Israelin kuninkaalle: minä olen niinkuin sinä, ja minun kansani niinkuin sinun kansas, ja minun hevoseni niinkuin sinun hevoses.
verses.king_james: And he said unto Jehoshaphat, Wilt thou go with me to battle to Ramoth–gilead? And Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel, I am as thou art, my people as thy people, my horses as thy horses.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Josaphat ad regem Israël : Sicut ego sum, ita et tu : populus meus et populus tuus unum sunt : et equites mei, equites tui. Dixitque Josaphat ad regem Israël : Quære, oro te, hodie sermonem Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְהוֹשָׁפָ֖ט אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל דְּרׇשׁ־נָ֥א כַיּ֖וֹם אֶת־דְּבַ֥ר יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Josaphat said to the king of Israel: As I am, so art thou: my people and thy people are one: and my horsemen are thy horsemen. And Josaphat said to the king of Israel: Inquire, I beseech thee, this day the word of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josaphat sanoi vielä Israelin kuninkaalle: kysy siis tänäpänä Herran sanaa!
verses.king_james: And Jehoshaphat said unto the king of Israel, Enquire, I pray thee, at the word of the Lord to day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Congregavit ergo rex Israël prophetas, quadringentos circiter viros, et ait ad eos : Ire debeo in Ramoth Galaad ad bellandum, an quiescere ? Qui responderunt : Ascende, et dabit eam Dominus in manu regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְבֹּ֨ץ מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֥ל אֶֽת־הַנְּבִיאִים֮ כְּאַרְבַּ֣ע מֵא֣וֹת אִישׁ֒ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֲלֵהֶ֗ם הַאֵלֵ֞ךְ עַל־רָמֹ֥ת גִּלְעָ֛ד לַמִּלְחָמָ֖ה אִם־אֶחְדָּ֑ל וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ עֲלֵ֔ה וְיִתֵּ֥ן אֲדֹנָ֖י בְּיַ֥ד הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then the king of Israel assembled the prophets, about four hundred men, and he said to them: Shall I go to Ramoth Galaad to fight, or shall I forbear? They answered: Go up, and the Lord will deliver it into the hand of the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Israelin kuningas kokosi prophetaita liki neljäsataa miestä ja sanoi heille: pitääkö minun menemän Gileadin Ramotiin sotaan, taikka lakkaaman? He sanoivat: mene, ja Herra antaa sen kuninkaan käsiin.
verses.king_james: Then the king of Israel gathered the prophets together, about four hundred men, and said unto them, Shall I go against Ramoth–gilead to battle, or shall I forbear? And they said, Go up; for the Lord shall deliver it into the hand of the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Josaphat : Non est hic propheta Domini quispiam, ut interrogemus per eum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ יְה֣וֹשָׁפָ֔ט הַאֵ֨ין פֹּ֥ה נָבִ֛יא לַֽיהֹוָ֖ה ע֑וֹד וְנִדְרְשָׁ֖ה מֵאֹתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Josaphat said: Is there not here some prophet of the Lord, that we may inquire by him?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Josaphat sanoi: eikö tässä ole vielä Herran prophetaa, kysyäksemme häneltä?
verses.king_james: And Jehoshaphat said, Is there not here a prophet of the Lord besides, that we might enquire of him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait rex Israël ad Josaphat : Remansit vir unus per quem possumus interrogare Dominum : sed ego odi eum, quia non prophetat mihi bonum, sed malum : Michæas filius Jemla. Cui Josaphat ait : Ne loquaris ita, rex.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֣ל ׀ אֶֽל־יְהוֹשָׁפָ֡ט ע֣וֹד אִישׁ־אֶחָ֡ד לִדְרֹשׁ֩ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֨ה מֵאֹת֜וֹ וַאֲנִ֣י שְׂנֵאתִ֗יו כִּ֠י לֹא־יִתְנַבֵּ֨א עָלַ֥י טוֹב֙ כִּ֣י אִם־רָ֔ע מִיכָ֖יְהוּ בֶּן־יִמְלָ֑ה וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ יְה֣וֹשָׁפָ֔ט אַל־יֹאמַ֥ר הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ כֵּֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Israel said to Josaphat. There is one man left, by whom we may inquire of the Lord; Micheas, the son of Jemla: but I hate him, for he doth not prophecy good to me, but evil. And Josaphat said: Speak not so, O king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin kuningas sanoi Josaphatille: vielä on yksi mies, Miika Jimlan poika, jolta kysyttäköön Herraa, mutta minä vihaan häntä; sillä ei hän minulle mitään hyvää ennusta, vaan pahaa. Josaphat sanoi: älköön kuningas niin puhuko.
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, There is yet one man, Micaiah the son of Imlah, by whom we may enquire of the Lord: but I hate him; for he doth not prophesy good concerning me, but evil. And Jehoshaphat said, Let not the king say so.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocavit ergo rex Israël eunuchum quemdam, et dixit ei : Festina adducere Michæam filium Jemla.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָא֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֶל־סָרִ֖יס אֶחָ֑ד וַיֹּ֕אמֶר מַהֲרָ֖ה מִיכָ֥יְהוּ בֶן־יִמְלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then the king of Israel called an eunuch, and said to him: Make haste, and bring hither Micheas, the son of Jemla.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Israelin kuningas kutsui yhden palvelian ja sanoi: tuo Miika Jimlan poika kiiruusti tänne!
verses.king_james: Then the king of Israel called an officer, and said, Hasten hither Micaiah the son of Imlah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rex autem Israël, et Josaphat rex Juda, sedebant unusquisque in solio suo, vestiti cultu regio, in area juxta ostium portæ Samariæ : et universi prophetæ prophetabant in conspectu eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֡ל וִיהוֹשָׁפָ֣ט מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֡ה יֹשְׁבִים֩ אִ֨ישׁ עַל־כִּסְא֜וֹ מְלֻבָּשִׁ֤ים בְּגָדִים֙ בְּגֹ֔רֶן פֶּ֖תַח שַׁ֣עַר שֹׁמְר֑וֹן וְכׇ֨ל־הַנְּבִיאִ֔ים מִֽתְנַבְּאִ֖ים לִפְנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Israel, and Josaphat, king of Juda, sat each on his throne, clothed with royal robes, in a court, by the entrance of the gate of Samaria, and all the prophets prophesied before them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Israelin kuningas ja Josaphat Juudan kuningas istuivat molemmat kuninkaallisella istuimellansa, vaatetettuina (kuninkaallisiin) vaatteisiin Samarian portin raitilla; ja kaikki prophetat ennustivat heidän edessänsä.
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah sat each on his throne, having put on their robes, in a void place in the entrance of the gate of Samaria; and all the prophets prophesied before them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fecit quoque sibi Sedecias filius Chanaana cornua ferrea, et ait : Hæc dicit Dominus : His ventilabis Syriam, donec deleas eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַשׂ ל֛וֹ צִדְקִיָּ֥ה בֶֽן־כְּנַעֲנָ֖ה קַרְנֵ֣י בַרְזֶ֑ל וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ כֹּה־אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה בְּאֵ֛לֶּה תְּנַגַּ֥ח אֶת־אֲרָ֖ם עַד־כַּלֹּתָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Sedecias, the son of Chanaana, made himself horns of iron, and said: Thus saith the Lord: With these shalt thou push Syria, till thou destroy it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Zedekia Kenanan poika oli tehnyt itsellensä rautasarvet, ja sanoi: näin sanoo Herra: puske näillä Syrialaisia siihenasti kuin he kokonansa hävitetään.
verses.king_james: And Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah made him horns of iron: and he said, Thus saith the Lord, With these shalt thou push the Syrians, until thou have consumed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnesque prophetæ similiter prophetabant, dicentes : Ascende in Ramoth Galaad, et vade prospere, et tradet Dominus in manus regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכׇ֨ל־הַנְּבִאִ֔ים נִבְּאִ֥ים כֵּ֖ן לֵאמֹ֑ר עֲלֵ֞ה רָמֹ֤ת גִּלְעָד֙ וְהַצְלַ֔ח וְנָתַ֥ן יְהֹוָ֖ה בְּיַ֥ד הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all the prophets prophesied in like manner, saying: Go up to Ramoth Galaad, and prosper, for the Lord will deliver it into the king's hands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin myös kaikki prophetat ennustivat ja sanoivat: mene Gileadin Ramotiin, ja sinä menestyt, Jumala antaa sen kuninkaan käsiin.
verses.king_james: And all the prophets prophesied so, saying, Go up to Ramoth–gilead, and prosper: for the Lord shall deliver it into the king’s hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nuntius vero qui ierat ut vocaret Michæam, locutus est ad eum, dicens : Ecce sermones prophetarum ore uno regi bona prædicant : sit ergo sermo tuus similis eorum, et loquere bona.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַמַּלְאָ֞ךְ אֲשֶׁר־הָלַ֣ךְ ׀ לִקְרֹ֣א מִיכָ֗יְהוּ דִּבֶּ֤ר אֵלָיו֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר הִנֵּה־נָ֞א דִּבְרֵ֧י הַנְּבִיאִ֛ים פֶּה־אֶחָ֥ד ט֖וֹב אֶל־הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ יְהִי־נָ֣א (דבריך) [דְבָרְךָ֗] כִּדְבַ֛ר אַחַ֥ד מֵהֶ֖ם וְדִבַּ֥רְתָּ טּֽוֹב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the messenger that went to call Micheas, spoke to him, saying: Behold the words of the prophets with one mouth declare good things to the king: let thy word, therefore, be like to theirs, and speak that which is good.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta sanansaattaja, joka oli mennyt Miikaa kutsumaan, puhui hänelle, sanoen: katso, kaikki prophetat ennustavat yhdestä suusta kuninkaalle hyvää: niin olkoon sinun sanas niinkuin heidänkin sanansa, ja puhu hyvää.
verses.king_james: And the messenger that was gone to call Micaiah spake unto him, saying, Behold now, the words of the prophets declare good unto the king with one mouth: let thy word, I pray thee, be like the word of one of them, and speak that which is good.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui Michæas ait : Vivit Dominus, quia quodcumque dixerit mihi Dominus, hoc loquar.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר מִיכָ֑יְהוּ חַי־יְהֹוָ֕ה כִּ֠י אֶת־אֲשֶׁ֨ר יֹאמַ֧ר יְהֹוָ֛ה אֵלַ֖י אֹת֥וֹ אֲדַבֵּֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Micheas said to him: As the Lord liveth, whatsoever the Lord shall say to me, that will I speak.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Miika sanoi: niin totta kuin Herra elää, mitä Herra sanoo minulle, sitä minä puhun.
verses.king_james: And Micaiah said, As the Lord liveth, what the Lord saith unto me, that will I speak.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit itaque ad regem, et ait illi rex : Michæa, ire debemus in Ramoth Galaad ad præliandum, an cessare ? Cui ille respondit : Ascende, et vade prospere, et tradet eam Dominus in manus regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבוֹא֮ אֶל־הַמֶּ֒לֶךְ֒ וַיֹּ֨אמֶר הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ אֵלָ֗יו מִיכָ֙יְהוּ֙ הֲנֵלֵ֞ךְ אֶל־רָמֹ֥ת גִּלְעָ֛ד לַמִּלְחָמָ֖ה אִם־נֶחְדָּ֑ל וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָיו֙ עֲלֵ֣ה וְהַצְלַ֔ח וְנָתַ֥ן יְהֹוָ֖ה בְּיַ֥ד הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So he came to the king, and the king said to him: Micheas, shall we go to Ramoth Galaad to battle, or shall we forbear? He answered him: Go up, and prosper, and the Lord shall deliver it into the king's hands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kun Miika tuli kuninkaan tykö, sanoi kuningas hänelle: Miika, menemmekö sotimaan Gileadin Ramotiin, taikka lakkaammeko? Hän sanoi hänelle: mene, ja sinä menestyt; sillä Herra antaa sen kuninkaan käsiin.
verses.king_james: So he came to the king. And the king said unto him, Micaiah, shall we go against Ramoth–gilead to battle, or shall we forbear? And he answered him, Go, and prosper: for the Lord shall deliver it into the hand of the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem rex ad eum : Iterum atque iterum adjuro te, ut non loquaris mihi nisi quod verum est, in nomine Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָיו֙ הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ עַד־כַּמֶּ֥ה פְעָמִ֖ים אֲנִ֣י מַשְׁבִּיעֶ֑ךָ אֲ֠שֶׁ֠ר לֹֽא־תְדַבֵּ֥ר אֵלַ֛י רַק־אֱמֶ֖ת בְּשֵׁ֥ם יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the king said to him: I adjure thee again and again, that thou tell me nothing but that which is true, in the name of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas sanoi taas hänelle: kuinka usein minun pitää sinua vannottaman, ettes minulle muuta sanoisi, kuin totuuden Herran nimeen?
verses.king_james: And the king said unto him, How many times shall I adjure thee that thou tell me nothing but that which is true in the name of the Lord?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ille ait : Vidi cunctum Israël dispersum in montibus, quasi oves non habentes pastorem. Et ait Dominus : Non habent isti dominum : revertatur unusquisque in domum suam in pace.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר רָאִ֤יתִי אֶת־כׇּל־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ נְפֹצִ֣ים אֶל־הֶהָרִ֔ים כַּצֹּ֕אן אֲשֶׁ֥ר אֵין־לָהֶ֖ם רֹעֶ֑ה וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יְהֹוָה֙ לֹֽא־אֲדֹנִ֣ים לָאֵ֔לֶּה יָשׁ֥וּבוּ אִישׁ־לְבֵית֖וֹ בְּשָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: I saw all Israel scattered upon the hills, like sheep that have no shepherd; and the Lord said: These have no master: let every man of them return to his house in peace.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: minä näin koko Israelin hajoitetuksi vuorille niinkuin lampaat, joilla ei paimenta ole. Ja Herra sanoi: ei näillä ole yhtään isäntää, palatkaan jokainen kotiansa rauhassa.
verses.king_james: And he said, I saw all Israel scattered upon the hills, as sheep that have not a shepherd: and the Lord said, These have no master: let them return every man to his house in peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: (Dixit ergo rex Israël ad Josaphat : Numquid non dixi tibi, quia non prophetat mihi bonum, sed semper malum ?)
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל אֶל־יְהוֹשָׁפָ֑ט הֲלוֹא֙ אָמַ֣רְתִּי אֵלֶ֔יךָ לוֹא־יִתְנַבֵּ֥א עָלַ֛י ט֖וֹב כִּ֥י אִם־רָֽע׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: (Then the king of Israel said to Josaphat: Did I not tell thee, that he prophesieth no good to me, but always evil?)
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin kuningas sanoi Josaphatille: enkö minä sinulle sanonut, ettei hän ennusta minulle hyvää, vaan pahaa?
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, Did I not tell thee that he would prophesy no good concerning me, but evil?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ille vero addens, ait : Propterea audi sermonem Domini : vidi Dominum sedentem super solium suum, et omnem exercitum cæli assistentem ei a dextris et a sinistris :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר לָכֵ֖ן שְׁמַ֣ע דְּבַר־יְהֹוָ֑ה רָאִ֤יתִי אֶת־יְהֹוָה֙ יֹשֵׁ֣ב עַל־כִּסְא֔וֹ וְכׇל־צְבָ֤א הַשָּׁמַ֙יִם֙ עֹמֵ֣ד עָלָ֔יו מִֽימִינ֖וֹ וּמִשְּׂמֹאלֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he added and said: Hear thou, therefore, the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord sitting on his throne, and all the army of heaven standing by him on the right hand and on the left:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: kuule siis Herran sanaa: minä näin Herran istuvan istuimellansa, ja koko taivaallisen sotajoukon seisovan hänen tykönänsä, oikialla ja vasemmalla puolella.
verses.king_james: And he said, Hear thou therefore the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord sitting on his throne, and all the host of heaven standing by him on his right hand and on his left.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ait Dominus : Quis decipiet Achab regem Israël, ut ascendat, et cadat in Ramoth Galaad ? Et dixit unus verba hujuscemodi, et alius aliter.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יְהֹוָ֗ה מִ֤י יְפַתֶּה֙ אֶת־אַחְאָ֔ב וְיַ֕עַל וְיִפֹּ֖ל בְּרָמֹ֣ת גִּלְעָ֑ד וַיֹּ֤אמֶר זֶה֙ בְּכֹ֔ה וְזֶ֥ה אֹמֵ֖ר בְּכֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said: Who shall deceive Achab, king of Israel, that he may go up, and fall at Ramoth Galaad? And one spoke words of this manner, and another otherwise.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi: kuka pettäis Ahabin, että hän menis ja lankeis Gileadin Ramotissa? Yksi sanoi niin ja toinen näin.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said, Who shall persuade Ahab, that he may go up and fall at Ramoth–gilead? And one said on this manner, and another said on that manner.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressus est autem spiritus, et stetit coram Domino, et ait : Ego decipiam illum. Cui locutus est Dominus : In quo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצֵ֣א הָר֗וּחַ וַֽיַּעֲמֹד֙ לִפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֔ה וַיֹּ֖אמֶר אֲנִ֣י אֲפַתֶּ֑נּוּ וַיֹּ֧אמֶר יְהֹוָ֛ה אֵלָ֖יו בַּמָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And there came forth a spirit, and stood before the Lord, and said: I will deceive him. And the Lord said to him: By what means?
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin läksi henki, joka seisoi Herran edessä, ja sanoi: minä petän hänen. Jolle Herra sanoi: missä?
verses.king_james: And there came forth a spirit, and stood before the Lord, and said, I will persuade him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ille ait : Egrediar, et ero spiritus mendax in ore omnium prophetarum ejus. Et dixit Dominus : Decipies, et prævalebis : egredere, et fac ita.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר אֵצֵא֙ וְהָיִ֙יתִי֙ ר֣וּחַ שֶׁ֔קֶר בְּפִ֖י כׇּל־נְבִיאָ֑יו וַיֹּ֗אמֶר תְּפַתֶּה֙ וְגַם־תּוּכָ֔ל צֵ֖א וַעֲשֵׂה־כֵֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: I will go forth, and be a lying spirit, in the mouth of all his prophets. And the Lord said: Thou shalt deceive him, and shalt prevail: go forth, and do so.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän vastasi: minä menen ja olen valheen henki kaikkein hänen prophetainsa suussa. Hän sanoi: petä häntä, ja sinä taidat, mene ja tee niin.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto him, Wherewith? And he said, I will go forth, and I will be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And he said, Thou shalt persuade him, and prevail also: go forth, and do so.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc igitur ecce dedit Dominus spiritum mendacii in ore omnium prophetarum tuorum, qui hic sunt, et Dominus locutus est contra te malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֗ה הִנֵּ֨ה נָתַ֤ן יְהֹוָה֙ ר֣וּחַ שֶׁ֔קֶר בְּפִ֖י כׇּל־נְבִיאֶ֣יךָ אֵ֑לֶּה וַֽיהֹוָ֔ה דִּבֶּ֥ר עָלֶ֖יךָ רָעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now, therefore, behold the Lord hath given a lying spirit in the mouth of all thy prophets that are here, and the Lord hath spoken evil against thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja katso, nyt Herra antoi valheen hengen kaikkein näiden sinun prophetais suuhun, ja Herra on puhunut sinua vastaan pahaa.
verses.king_james: Now therefore, behold, the Lord hath put a lying spirit in the mouth of all these thy prophets, and the Lord hath spoken evil concerning thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accessit autem Sedecias filius Chanaana, et percussit Michæam in maxillam, et dixit : Mene ergo dimisit spiritus Domini, et locutus est tibi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגַּשׁ֙ צִדְקִיָּ֣הוּ בֶֽן־כְּנַעֲנָ֔ה וַיַּכֶּ֥ה אֶת־מִיכָ֖יְהוּ עַל־הַלֶּ֑חִי וַיֹּ֕אמֶר אֵי־זֶ֨ה עָבַ֧ר רֽוּחַ־יְהֹוָ֛ה מֵאִתִּ֖י לְדַבֵּ֥ר אוֹתָֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Sedecias, the son of Chanaana, came, and struck Micheas on the cheek, and said: Hath then the spirit of the Lord left me, and spoken to thee?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin astui Zedekia Kenanan poika edes, ja löi Miikaa poskelle, ja sanoi: onko Herran henki paennut minusta sinua puhuttelemaan?
verses.king_james: But Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah went near, and smote Micaiah on the cheek, and said, Which way went the Spirit of the Lord from me to speak unto thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Michæas : Visurus es in die illa quando ingredieris cubiculum intra cubiculum ut abscondaris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר מִיכָ֔יְהוּ הִנְּךָ֥ רֹאֶ֖ה בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֑וּא אֲשֶׁ֥ר תָּבֹ֛א חֶ֥דֶר בְּחֶ֖דֶר לְהֵחָבֵֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Micheas said: Thou shalt see in the day when thou shalt go into a chamber within a chamber to hide thyself.
verses.finnish_biblia: Miika vastasi: katso, sinä olet sen näkevä sinä päivänä, jona käyt majasta majaan lymytäkses.
verses.king_james: And Micaiah said, Behold, thou shalt see in that day, when thou shalt go into an inner chamber to hide thyself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait rex Israël : Tollite Michæam, et maneat apud Amon principem civitatis, et apud Joas filium Amelech,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל קַ֚ח אֶת־מִיכָ֔יְהוּ וַהֲשִׁיבֵ֖הוּ אֶל־אָמֹ֣ן שַׂר־הָעִ֑יר וְאֶל־יוֹאָ֖שׁ בֶּן־הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Israel said: Take Micheas and let him abide with Amon, the governor of the city, and with Joas, the son of Amalech;
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Israelin kuningas: ota Miika ja vie häntä jälleen kaupungin päämiehen Amonin tykö ja Joaksen kuninkaan pojan tykö,
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel said, Take Micaiah, and carry him back unto Amon the governor of the city, and to Joash the king’s son;

verses.latin_vulgate: et dicite eis : Hæc dicit rex : Mittite virum istum in carcerem, et sustentate eum pane tribulationis, et aqua angustiæ, donec revertar in pace.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמַרְתָּ֗ כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ שִׂ֥ימוּ אֶת־זֶ֖ה בֵּ֣ית הַכֶּ֑לֶא וְהַאֲכִלֻ֜הוּ לֶ֤חֶם לַ֙חַץ֙ וּמַ֣יִם לַ֔חַץ עַ֖ד בֹּאִ֥י בְשָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And tell them: Thus saith the king: Put this man in prison, and feed him with bread of affliction, and water of distress till I return in peace.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sano: näin sanoo kuningas: pankaat tämä vankihuoneesen, ja ruokkikaat häntä murheen leivällä ja murheen vedellä, siihen asti kuin minä tulen rauhassa.
verses.king_james: And say, Thus saith the king, Put this fellow in the prison, and feed him with bread of affliction and with water of affliction, until I come in peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Michæas : Si reversus fueris in pace, non est locutus in me Dominus. Et ait : Audite, populi omnes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר מִיכָ֔יְהוּ אִם־שׁ֤וֹב תָּשׁוּב֙ בְּשָׁל֔וֹם לֹא־דִבֶּ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה בִּ֑י וַיֹּ֕אמֶר שִׁמְע֖וּ עַמִּ֥ים כֻּלָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Micheas said: If thou return in peace, the Lord hath not spoken by me. And he said: Hear, all ye people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Miika sanoi: jos sinä toki palajat rauhassa, niin ei Herra ole puhunut minun kauttani; ja sanoi: kuulkaat, kaikki kansat!
verses.king_james: And Micaiah said, If thou return at all in peace, the Lord hath not spoken by me. And he said, Hearken, O people, every one of you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendit itaque rex Israël, et Josaphat rex Juda, in Ramoth Galaad.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֧עַל מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל וִיהוֹשָׁפָ֥ט מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֖ה רָמֹ֥ת גִּלְעָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: So the king of Israel, and Josaphat, king of Juda, went up to Ramoth-Galaad.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin kuningas ja Josaphat, Juudan kuningas, meni ylös Gileadin Ramotiin.
verses.king_james: So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah went up to Ramoth–gilead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit itaque rex Israël ad Josaphat : Sume arma, et ingredere prælium, et induere vestibus tuis. Porro rex Israël mutavit habitum suum, et ingressus est bellum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ מֶ֨לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל אֶל־יְהוֹשָׁפָ֗ט הִתְחַפֵּשׂ֙ וָבֹ֣א בַמִּלְחָמָ֔ה וְאַתָּ֖ה לְבַ֣שׁ בְּגָדֶ֑יךָ וַיִּתְחַפֵּשׂ֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וַיָּב֖וֹא בַּמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Israel said to Josaphat: Take thy armour, and go into the battle, and put on thy own garments. But the king of Israel changed his dress, and went into the battle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin kuningas sanoi Josaphatille, koska hän muutti vaatteensa sotaan lähtiessänsä: pue sinä itses omiin vaatteisiis. Ja Israelin kuningas muutti vaatteensa ja meni sotaan.
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, I will disguise myself, and enter into the battle; but put thou on thy robes. And the king of Israel disguised himself, and went into the battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rex autem Syriæ præceperat principibus curruum triginta duobus, dicens : Non pugnabitis contra minorem et majorem quempiam, nisi contra regem Israël solum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמֶ֣לֶךְ אֲרָ֡ם צִוָּ֣ה אֶת־שָׂרֵי֩ הָרֶ֨כֶב אֲשֶׁר־ל֜וֹ שְׁלֹשִׁ֤ים וּשְׁנַ֙יִם֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר לֹ֚א תִּלָּ֣חֲמ֔וּ אֶת־קָטֹ֖ן וְאֶת־גָּד֑וֹל כִּ֛י אִֽם־אֶת־מֶ֥לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל לְבַדּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Syria had commanded the two and thirty captains of the chariots, saying: You shall not fight against any, small or great, but against the king of Israel only.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Syrian kuningas käski vaunuin päämiehille, joita hänellä oli kaksineljättäkymmentä, sanoen: ei teidän pidä sotiman pientä eli suurta vastaan, mutta ainoastansa Israelin kuningasta vastaan.
verses.king_james: But the king of Syria commanded his thirty and two captains that had rule over his chariots, saying, Fight neither with small nor great, save only with the king of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo vidissent principes curruum Josaphat, suspicati sunt quod ipse esset rex Israël, et impetu facto pugnabant contra eum : et exclamavit Josaphat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֡י כִּרְאוֹת֩ שָׂרֵ֨י הָרֶ֜כֶב אֶת־יְהוֹשָׁפָ֗ט וְהֵ֤מָּה אָֽמְרוּ֙ אַ֣ךְ מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֣ל ה֔וּא וַיָּסֻ֥רוּ עָלָ֖יו לְהִלָּחֵ֑ם וַיִּזְעַ֖ק יְהוֹשָׁפָֽט׃
verses.douay_rheims: So when the captains of the chariots saw Josaphat, they suspected that he was the king of Israel, and making a violent assault, they fought against him: and Josaphat cried out.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska vaunuin päämiehet näkivät Josaphatin, sanoivat he: tämä on tosin Israelin kuningas, ja poikkesivat sotimaan häntä vastaan. Niin Josaphat huusi.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, that they said, Surely it is the king of Israel. And they turned aside to fight against him: and Jehoshaphat cried out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intellexeruntque principes curruum quod non esset rex Israël, et cessaverunt ab eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י כִּרְאוֹת֙ שָׂרֵ֣י הָרֶ֔כֶב כִּי־לֹא־מֶ֥לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל ה֑וּא וַיָּשׁ֖וּבוּ מֵאַחֲרָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the captains of the chariots perceived that he was not the king of Israel, and they turned away from him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska vaunuin päämiehet näkivät, ettei se ollut Israelin kuningas, luopuivat he hänestä.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when the captains of the chariots perceived that it was not the king of Israel, that they turned back from pursuing him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir autem quidam tetendit arcum, in incertum sagittam dirigens, et casu percussit regem Israël inter pulmonem et stomachum. At ille dixit aurigæ suo : Verte manum tuam, et ejice me de exercitu, quia graviter vulneratus sum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִ֗ישׁ מָשַׁ֤ךְ בַּקֶּ֙שֶׁת֙ לְתֻמּ֔וֹ וַיַּכֶּה֙ אֶת־מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל בֵּ֥ין הַדְּבָקִ֖ים וּבֵ֣ין הַשִּׁרְיָ֑ן וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לְרַכָּב֗וֹ הֲפֹ֥ךְ יָדְךָ֛ וְהוֹצִיאֵ֥נִי מִן־הַֽמַּחֲנֶ֖ה כִּ֥י הׇחֳלֵֽיתִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And a certain man bent his bow, shooting at a venture, and chanced to strike the king of Israel, between the lungs and the stomach. But he said to the driver of his chariot: Turn thy hand, and carry me out of the army, for I am grievously wounded.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta yksi mies jännitti joutsensa yksinkertaisuudessa ja ampui Israelin kuningasta rautapaidan jatkoon. Ja hän sanoi vaunumiehellensä: käännä kätes ja vie minua pois leiristä; sillä minä olen haavoitettu.
verses.king_james: And a certain man drew a bow at a venture, and smote the king of Israel between the joints of the harness: wherefore he said unto the driver of his chariot, Turn thine hand, and carry me out of the host; for I am wounded.

verses.latin_vulgate: Commissum est ergo prælium in die illa, et rex Israël stabat in curru suo contra Syros, et mortuus est vespere : fluebat autem sanguis plagæ in sinum currus,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּעֲלֶ֤ה הַמִּלְחָמָה֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא וְהַמֶּ֗לֶךְ הָיָ֧ה מׇעֳמָ֛ד בַּמֶּרְכָּבָ֖ה נֹ֣כַח אֲרָ֑ם וַיָּ֣מׇת בָּעֶ֔רֶב וַיִּ֥צֶק דַּֽם־הַמַּכָּ֖ה אֶל־חֵ֥יק הָרָֽכֶב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the battle was fought that day, and the king of Israel stood in his chariot against the Syrians, and he died in the evening: and the blood ran out of the wound into the midst of the chariot.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sinä päivänä oli suuri sota, ja kuningas seisoi vaunuissansa Syrialaisia vastaan, ja kuoli ehtoona, ja veri vuoti hänen haavastansa vaunuin keskelle.
verses.king_james: And the battle increased that day: and the king was stayed up in his chariot against the Syrians, and died at even: and the blood ran out of the wound into the midst of the chariot.

verses.latin_vulgate: et præco insonuit in universo exercitu antequam sol occumberet, dicens : Unusquisque revertatur in civitatem, et in terram suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲבֹ֤ר הָֽרִנָּה֙ בַּֽמַּחֲנֶ֔ה כְּבֹ֥א הַשֶּׁ֖מֶשׁ לֵאמֹ֑ר אִ֥ישׁ אֶל־עִיר֖וֹ וְאִ֥ישׁ אֶל־אַרְצֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the herald proclaimed through all the army, before the sun set, saying: Let every man return to his own city, and to his own country.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja auringon laskiessa kuulutettiin leirissä sanoen: menkään jokainen kaupunkiinsa ja maallensa!
verses.king_james: And there went a proclamation throughout the host about the going down of the sun, saying, Every man to his city, and every man to his own country.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mortuus est autem rex, et perlatus est in Samariam : sepelieruntque regem in Samaria,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣מׇת הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וַיָּב֖וֹא שֹׁמְר֑וֹן וַיִּקְבְּר֥וּ אֶת־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ בְּשֹׁמְרֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king died, and was carried into Samaria: and they buried the king in Samaria.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas kuoli ja vietiin Samariaan, ja he hautasivat kuninkaan Samariassa.
verses.king_james: So the king died, and was brought to Samaria; and they buried the king in Samaria.

verses.latin_vulgate: et laverunt currum ejus in piscina Samariæ : et linxerunt canes sanguinem ejus, et habenas laverunt, juxta verbum Domini quod locutus fuerat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁטֹ֨ף אֶת־הָרֶ֜כֶב עַ֣ל ׀ בְּרֵכַ֣ת שֹׁמְר֗וֹן וַיָּלֹ֤קּוּ הַכְּלָבִים֙ אֶת־דָּמ֔וֹ וְהַזֹּנ֖וֹת רָחָ֑צוּ כִּדְבַ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבֵּֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they washed his chariot in the pool of Samaria and the dogs licked up his blood, and they washed the reins according to the word of the Lord which he had spoken.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuin vaunut virutettiin Samarian kalalammikossa, nuolivat koirat hänen vertansa, ja portot pesivät, Herran sanan jälkeen, jonka hän puhui.
verses.king_james: And one washed the chariot in the pool of Samaria; and the dogs licked up his blood; and they washed his armour; according unto the word of the Lord which he spake.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliqua autem sermonum Achab, et universa quæ fecit, et domus eburnea quam ædificavit, cunctarumque urbium quas exstruxit, nonne hæc scripta sunt in libro sermonum dierum regum Israël ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֶ֩תֶר֩ דִּבְרֵ֨י אַחְאָ֜ב וְכׇל־אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשָׂ֗ה וּבֵ֤ית הַשֵּׁן֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בָּנָ֔ה וְכׇל־הֶעָרִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֣ר בָּנָ֑ה הֲלוֹא־הֵ֣ם כְּתוּבִ֗ים עַל־סֵ֛פֶר דִּבְרֵ֥י הַיָּמִ֖ים לְמַלְכֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the rest of the acts of Achab, and all that he did, and the house of ivory that he made, and all the cities that he built, are they not written in the book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä Ahabista enempi on sanomista, ja kaikista hänen töistänsä, ja huoneesta, jonka hän elephantin luista rakensi, ja kaikista kaupungeista, jotka hän rakensi: eikö ne ole kirjoitetut Israelin kuningasten aikakirjassa?
verses.king_james: Now the rest of the acts of Ahab, and all that he did, and the ivory house which he made, and all the cities that he built, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dormivit ergo Achab cum patribus suis, et regnavit Ochozias filius ejus pro eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכַּ֥ב אַחְאָ֖ב עִם־אֲבֹתָ֑יו וַיִּמְלֹ֛ךְ אֲחַזְיָ֥הוּ בְנ֖וֹ תַּחְתָּֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: So Achab slept with his fathers; and Ochozias, his son, reigned in his stead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin Ahab nukkui isäinsä kanssa, ja hänen poikansa Ahasia tuli kuninkaaksi hänen siaansa.
verses.king_james: So Ahab slept with his fathers; and Ahaziah his son reigned in his stead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Josaphat vero filius Asa regnare cœperat super Judam anno quarto Achab regis Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וִיהֽוֹשָׁפָט֙ בֶּן־אָסָ֔א מָלַ֖ךְ עַל־יְהוּדָ֑ה בִּשְׁנַ֣ת אַרְבַּ֔ע לְאַחְאָ֖ב מֶ֥לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Josaphat, the son of Asa, began to reign over Juda, in the fourth year of Acbab, king of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Josaphat Asan poika Juudan kuninkaaksi neljäntenä Ahabin Israelin kuninkaan vuonna.
verses.king_james: And Jehoshaphat the son of Asa began to reign over Judah in the fourth year of Ahab king of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Triginta quinque annorum erat cum regnare cœpisset, et viginti quinque annis regnavit in Jerusalem : nomen matris ejus Azuba filia Salai.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהוֹשָׁפָ֗ט בֶּן־שְׁלֹשִׁ֨ים וְחָמֵ֤שׁ שָׁנָה֙ בְּמׇלְכ֔וֹ וְעֶשְׂרִ֤ים וְחָמֵשׁ֙ שָׁנָ֔ה מָלַ֖ךְ בִּירוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם וְשֵׁ֣ם אִמּ֔וֹ עֲזוּבָ֖ה בַּת־שִׁלְחִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: He was five and thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned five and twenty years in Jerusalem: the name of his mother was Azuba, the daughter of Salai.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josaphat oli viiden ajastajan neljättäkymmentä vanha ruvetessansa hallitsemaan, ja hallitsi viisikolmattakymmentä ajastaikaa Jerusalemissa. Ja hänen äitinsä nimi oli Ashuba Silhin tytär.
verses.king_james: Jehoshaphat was thirty and five years old when he began to reign; and he reigned twenty and five years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Azubah the daughter of Shilhi.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ambulavit in omni via Asa patris sui, et non declinavit ex ea : fecitque quod rectum erat in conspectu Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֗לֶךְ בְּכׇל־דֶּ֛רֶךְ אָסָ֥א אָבִ֖יו לֹא־סָ֣ר מִמֶּ֑נּוּ לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת הַיָּשָׁ֖ר בְּעֵינֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he walked in all the way of Asa, his father, and he declined not from it: and he did that which was right in the sight of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän vaelsi kaikissa isänsä Asan teissä, eikä poikennut niistä, ja teki mitä Herralle kelpasi.
verses.king_james: And he walked in all the ways of Asa his father; he turned not aside from it, doing that which was right in the eyes of the Lord: nevertheless the high places were not taken away; for the people offered and burnt incense yet in the high places.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pacemque habuit Josaphat cum rege Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּשְׁלֵ֥ם יְהוֹשָׁפָ֖ט עִם־מֶ֥לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Josaphat had peace with the king of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta ei hän pannut pois korkeuksia, joissa kansa vielä uhrasi ja suitsutti.
verses.king_james: And Jehoshaphat made peace with the king of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliqua autem verborum Josaphat, et opera ejus quæ gessit, et prælia, nonne hæc scripta sunt in libro verborum dierum regum Juda ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֶ֨תֶר דִּבְרֵ֧י יְהוֹשָׁפָ֛ט וּגְבוּרָת֥וֹ אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֖ה וַאֲשֶׁ֣ר נִלְחָ֑ם הֲלֹא־הֵ֣ם כְּתוּבִ֗ים עַל־סֵ֛פֶר דִּבְרֵ֥י הַיָּמִ֖ים לְמַלְכֵ֥י יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the rest of the acts of Josaphat, and his works which he did, and his battles, are they not written in the book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josaphat teki rauhan Israelin kuninkaan kanssa.
verses.king_james: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, and his might that he shewed, and how he warred, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et reliquias effeminatorum qui remanserant in diebus Asa patris ejus, abstulit de terra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֶ֙תֶר֙ הַקָּדֵ֔שׁ אֲשֶׁ֣ר נִשְׁאַ֔ר בִּימֵ֖י אָסָ֣א אָבִ֑יו בִּעֵ֖ר מִן־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the remnant also of the effeminate, who remained in the days of Asa, his father, he took out of the land.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta muut Josaphatin sanat ja hänen voimansa ja työnsä, ja sotansa: eikö ne ole kirjoitetut Juudan kuningasten aikakirjassa?
verses.king_james: And the remnant of the sodomites, which remained in the days of his father Asa, he took out of the land.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nec erat tunc rex constitutus in Edom.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמֶ֥לֶךְ אֵ֛ין בֶּאֱד֖וֹם נִצָּ֥ב מֶֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And there was then no king appointed in Edom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja mitä huorintekijöistä jäänyt oli hänen isänsä Asan aikana, ne ajoi hän pois maalta.
verses.king_james: There was then no king in Edom: a deputy was king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rex vero Josaphat fecerat classes in mari, quæ navigarent in Ophir propter aurum : et ire non potuerunt, quia confractæ sunt in Asiongaber.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהוֹשָׁפָ֡ט (עשר) [עָשָׂה֩] אֳנִיּ֨וֹת תַּרְשִׁ֜ישׁ לָלֶ֧כֶת אֹפִ֛ירָה לַזָּהָ֖ב וְלֹ֣א הָלָ֑ךְ כִּֽי־[נִשְׁבְּר֥וּ] (נשברה) אֳנִיּ֖וֹת בְּעֶצְי֥וֹן גָּֽבֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: But king Josaphat made navies on the sea, to sail into Ophir for gold: but they could not go, for the ships were broken in Asiongaber.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja silloin ei ollut kuningasta Edomissa, vaan yksi oli hänen siassansa.
verses.king_james: Jehoshaphat made ships of Tharshish to go to Ophir for gold: but they went not; for the ships were broken at Ezion–geber.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc ait Ochozias filius Achab ad Josaphat : Vadant servi mei cum servis tuis in navibus. Et noluit Josaphat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֠ז אָמַ֞ר אֲחַזְיָ֤הוּ בֶן־אַחְאָב֙ אֶל־יְה֣וֹשָׁפָ֔ט יֵלְכ֧וּ עֲבָדַ֛י עִם־עֲבָדֶ֖יךָ בׇּאֳנִיּ֑וֹת וְלֹ֥א אָבָ֖ה יְהוֹשָׁפָֽט׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Ochozias, the son of Achab, said to Josaphat: Let my servants go with thy servants in the ships. And Josaphat would not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Josaphat oli antanut tehdä haaksia Tarsikseen, jotka piti Ophririin purjehtiman, kultaa noutamaan; mutta ei ne tulleet matkaan, vaan tulivat särjetyiksi EtseonGeberissä.
verses.king_james: Then said Ahaziah the son of Ahab unto Jehoshaphat, Let my servants go with thy servants in the ships. But Jehoshaphat would not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dormivitque Josaphat cum patribus suis, et sepultus est cum eis in civitate David patris sui : regnavitque Joram filius ejus pro eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכַּ֤ב יְהוֹשָׁפָט֙ עִם־אֲבֹתָ֔יו וַיִּקָּבֵר֙ עִם־אֲבֹתָ֔יו בְּעִ֖יר דָּוִ֣ד אָבִ֑יו וַיִּמְלֹ֛ךְ יְהוֹרָ֥ם בְּנ֖וֹ תַּחְתָּֽיו׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And Josaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with them in the city of David, his father: and Joram, his son, reigned in his stead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin Ahaisa Ahabin poika sanoi Josaphatille: anna minun palveliaini mennä sinun palveliais kanssa haaksiin; mutta ei Josaphat tahtonut.
verses.king_james: And Jehoshaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David his father: and Jehoram his son reigned in his stead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ochozias autem filius Achab regnare cœperat super Israël in Samaria, anno septimodecimo Josaphat regis Juda : regnavitque super Israël duobus annis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲחַזְיָ֣הוּ בֶן־אַחְאָ֗ב מָלַ֤ךְ עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ בְּשֹׁ֣מְר֔וֹן בִּשְׁנַת֙ שְׁבַ֣ע עֶשְׂרֵ֔ה לִיהוֹשָׁפָ֖ט מֶ֣לֶךְ יְהוּדָ֑ה וַיִּמְלֹ֥ךְ עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל שְׁנָתָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ochozias, the son of Achab, began to reign over Israel, in Samaria, in the seventeenth year of Josaphat, king of Juda, and he reigned over Israel two years.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Josaphat kuoli isäinsä kanssa ja haudattiin isänsä Davidin kaupunkiin. Ja Joram hallitsi hänen siassansa.
verses.king_james: Ahaziah the son of Ahab began to reign over Israel in Samaria the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, and reigned two years over Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et fecit malum in conspectu Domini, et ambulavit in via patris sui et matris suæ, et in via Jeroboam filii Nabat, qui peccare fecit Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַשׂ הָרַ֖ע בְּעֵינֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיֵּ֗לֶךְ בְּדֶ֤רֶךְ אָבִיו֙ וּבְדֶ֣רֶךְ אִמּ֔וֹ וּבְדֶ֙רֶךְ֙ יָרׇבְעָ֣ם בֶּן־נְבָ֔ט אֲשֶׁ֥ר הֶחֱטִ֖יא אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he did evil in the sight of the Lord, and walked in the way of his father and his mother, and in the way of Jeroboam, the son of Nabat, who made Israel to sin.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Ahasia Ahabin poika tuli Israelin kuninkaaksi Samariassa, Josaphatin kuninkaan seitsemäntenä vuonna toistakymmentä, ja hallitsi Israelia kaksi vuotta,
verses.king_james: And he did evil in the sight of the Lord, and walked in the way of his father, and in the way of his mother, and in the way of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel to sin:

verses.latin_vulgate: Servivit quoque Baal, et adoravit eum, et irritavit Dominum Deum Israël, juxta omnia quæ fecerat pater ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיַּעֲבֹד֙ אֶת־הַבַּ֔עַל וַיִּֽשְׁתַּחֲוֶ֖ה ל֑וֹ וַיַּכְעֵ֗ס אֶת־יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל כְּכֹ֥ל אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֖ה אָבִֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: He served also Baal, and worshipped him, and provoked the Lord, the God of Israel, according to all that his father had done.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja teki pahaa Herran edessä, ja vaelsi isänsä tiellä, ja äitinsä tiellä ja Jerobeamin Nebatin pojan tiellä, joka Israelin saatti syntiä tekemään. Ja hän palveli Baalia ja kumartaen rukoili häntä, ja vihoitti Herran Israelin Jumalan, kaiken sen jälkeen minkä hänen isänsä oli tehnyt.
verses.king_james: For he served Baal, and worshipped him, and provoked to anger the Lord God of Israel, according to all that his father had done.
